overview: The Village of Weston Plan Commission approved a site plan for the new Mountain Bay Metropolitan Police Department and Municipal Court facility at 3600 Community Center Drive, and adopted a salt storage shed inspection program for wellhead protection areas. Both items passed unanimously with conditions.

agenda.item: Call to order and roll call
Reordering of agenda to take item six first
Site plan review for Mountain Bay Metro Police Department and Municipal Court facility
Creation of salt storage shed inspection program for wellhead protection district
April 2026 staff approved certified survey maps and site plans
Planning and development department monthly project update report
Future agenda items and staff updates

discussions.item: Site plan review - Mountain Bay Metro Police Department and Municipal Court facility
discussions.body: Staff presented the site plan for the new police department and court facility at 3600 Community Center Drive. Chief Jeremy explained the building was rotated to face east due to a 36-inch gas pipeline running through the property, which significantly reduced fill costs. The building meets all B2 zoning requirements. Discussion included future expansion capability (possible to the north), placement of an alternate storage shed, and landscaping compliance. A motion to recommend approval to the board of trustees with conditions 2-5 passed unanimously.

discussions.item: Salt storage shed inspection program for wellhead protection district
discussions.body: Staff proposed a two-inspection-per-year program for salt storage facilities in wellhead protection areas - one warm weather inspection focused on structural integrity and one winter operational inspection. The program uses a standardized inspection worksheet similar to DOT standards. Staff noted one existing facility near Sternberg has never gone through the wellhead protection conditional use process and commission agreed staff should reach out to bring them into compliance. Motion to approve the program with reporting back to both Plan Commission and Public Works passed unanimously.
Motion to approve minutes as presented passed unanimously with no discussion.

Staff announced the first joint Plan Commission/Village Board steering committee meeting for the comprehensive plan is scheduled for Monday, June 1st at 5:00 p.m. Discussion included potential future residential TIF districts for workforce housing, with staff noting Ehlers has prepared a presentation on qualifications that could be shared with commissioners.

publicComment: Jim Pensel of 5002 Arrow Street, Weston, spoke via Zoom. He urged the commission to exercise common sense regarding landscaping requirements for the police department project, citing concerns about taxpayer costs similar to the Green Heck Turner building. He also requested the commission require annual reporting on the salt shed inspection program to ensure compliance visits are documented.
actionItems: Site plan for Mountain Bay Metro Police Department and Municipal Court facility recommended for approval to Board of Trustees with conditions 2-5
Salt storage shed inspection program adopted with twice-yearly inspections and annual reporting to Plan Commission and Public Works
Staff to reach out to salt storage facility near Sternberg that has not gone through wellhead protection conditional use process
Staff to send email and calendar invite for June 1st steering committee meeting for comprehensive plan
Electric vehicle charging stations added to future agenda (likely fall timeframe)
Next regular meeting scheduled for Monday, June 8, 2026 at 6:00 p.m.
title: Plan Commission
